basic_details.job_title: RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1853 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Railway Recruitment Cell (RRC), North Central Railway (NCR) has released a notification for engagement of Act Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Online applications are invited from eligible candidates for training against 1,853 slots across various trades for the year 2026-27.

application_fee.details: The application fee is Rs. 100/- (non-refundable). SC, ST, PwBD, and Female candidates are exempted from paying the application fee. The application fee must be paid online through the payment gateway during the online application process.

age_limit.details: General Candidates: 15 to 24 Years SC/ST Candidates: Upper age relaxed by 5 Years OBC Candidates: Upper age relaxed by 3 Years PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability): Upper age relaxed by 10 Years Ex-Servicemen: Upper age relaxed up to additional 10 Years (service rendered in Defence Forces + 3 years, subject to minimum 6 months continuous service)

selection_process: Merit List based on Qualifying Examination marks Document Verification Medical Examination
how_to_apply: Read all instructions in the notification carefully before filling the form.
Create a valid personal email ID if not already available, and maintain it till the end of the engagement process.
Register and fill in the online application form with accurate details.
Submit the application well before the closing date to avoid website load/jam issues.
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: RRC NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview
RRC/NCR Act Apprentice 2026 - Post Details
content_sections.text: Railway Recruitment Cell, North Central Railway has announced 1853 slots for engagement as Act Apprentices for training in designated trades. The posting locations include: (Trade-wise vacancy breakdown not available in the provided notification excerpt - refer to the official PDF for full trade-wise distribution.)

content_sections.heading: Who Can Apply for RRC/NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Candidates meeting the age and educational eligibility for the designated trades can apply. The eligibility details are as follows: Educational Qualification: Candidates must have passed SSC/Matriculation/ 10th Class or its equivalent (under the 10+2 system) with a minimum of 50% aggregate marks from a recognized Board. Candidates must possess an ITI Certificate/National Trade Certificate (NTC) in the relevant trade issued by NCVT/SCVT recognized by the Government of India. Possession of a valid NCVT/SCVT-affiliated ITI Certificate in the relevant trade is mandatory. Domicile: As per RRC/NCR notified criteria

content_sections.heading: RRC NCR Act Apprentice 2026 - Age Limit (as on 07.07.2026)
content_sections.text: General Candidates: 15 to 24 Years SC/ST Candidates: Upper age relaxed by 5 Years OBC Candidates: Upper age relaxed by 3 Years PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability): Upper age relaxed by 10 Years Ex-Servicemen: Upper age relaxed up to additional 10 Years (service rendered in Defence Forces + 3 years, subject to minimum 6 months continuous service)

content_sections.heading: RRC NCR Act Apprentice 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee is Rs. 100/- (non-refundable). SC, ST, PwBD, and Female candidates are exempted from paying the application fee. The application fee must be paid online through the payment gateway during the online application process.

content_sections.heading: RRC NCR Act Apprentice 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: Merit List based on Qualifying Examination marks Document Verification Medical Examination

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for RRC/NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official RRC/NCR website. Read all instructions in the notification carefully before filling the form. Create a valid personal email ID if not already available, and maintain it till the end of the engagement process. Register and fill in the online application form with accurate details. Upload required documents and certificates. Submit the application well before the closing date to avoid website load/jam issues. Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is RRC NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: RRC NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is a training opportunity released by the Railway Recruitment Cell (RRC), North Central Railway for engagement of 1,853 Act Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. Eligible candidates can apply online from 08 July 2026 to 07 August 2026. The recruitment is meant to provide apprenticeship training in various designated trades across different NCR divisions.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 1,853 Apprentice vacancies have been announced by the Railway Recruitment Cell, North Central Railway. These apprenticeship slots are available at the NCR Headquarters, Prayagraj, Agra Division, Jhansi Division, and Jhansi Workshop. Candidates should refer to the official notification for the complete trade-wise vacancy distribution.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for RRC NCR Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed 10th/Matriculation (SSC) with at least 50% aggregate marks from a recognized board. In addition, applicants must possess a National Trade Certificate (NTC) or ITI Certificate in the relevant trade issued by NCVT or SCVT recognized by the Government of India. Both qualifications are mandatory to be eligible.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for RRC NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age required is 15 years, while the maximum age is 24 years as on 07 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories, including SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen, as per Government of India rules. Candidates should verify their eligibility before applying.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee for General, OBC, and EWS candidates is ₹100, which is non-refundable. However, SC, ST, PwBD, and all Female candidates are exempted from paying the application fee. Payment must be completed online through the official application portal.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process is based entirely on a merit list prepared using the marks obtained in the 10th examination and the relevant ITI trade. Shortlisted candidates will be called for Document Verification followed by a Medical Examination. There is no written examination or interview.

faq.question: Q7. What are the important dates for RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts on 08 July 2026 and the last date to submit the application is 07 August 2026. Candidates should complete registration and fee payment before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Late applications will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply for RRC NCR Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need to visit the official RRC North Central Railway website and complete the online registration process. After filling in the application form, applicants must upload the required documents, pay the fee (if applicable), and submit the form before the closing date. It is advisable to keep a printed copy of the submitted application.

faq.question: Q9. Is an ITI certificate mandatory for RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, an ITI Certificate or National Trade Certificate (NTC) in the relevant trade is compulsory for all applicants. The certificate must be issued by NCVT or SCVT recognized by the Government of India. Candidates without the required ITI qualification are not eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q10. Is RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026 a permanent railway job?
faq.answer: No, the RRC NCR Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is not a permanent government job. It is an apprenticeship training program conducted under the Apprentices Act, 1961, to provide practical training and skill development. Completion of apprenticeship training does not guarantee permanent employment in the Indian Railways.
